description:
<!--
    USER-FACING TASK DESCRIPTION
    ----------------------------
    You are a developer who wants to keep a tidy project workspace by running
    a small maintenance script at regular intervals.  Configure everything
    so that the job is handled automatically by cron and prove that the cron‐
    table was actually updated.

    1.  Create the directory
            /home/user/project/maintenance
        if it does not already exist.

    2.  Inside that directory create an executable shell script called
            /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh
        whose *entire* contents (including the she-bang) are **exactly**:

            #!/bin/bash
            echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'): cleanup executed" >> /home/user/project/maintenance/maintenance.log

        • The script must be given executable permission for the owner
          so that cron can run it (e.g. mode 700 or 755 is fine).

    3.  Add the following single line to your personal crontab
            5 */6 * * * /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh >> /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.log 2>&1
        Notes:
        • Do not surround the entry with comments or blank lines.
        • Make sure **one and only one** copy of that line ends up in the final crontab.

    4.  After installing the new crontab, export its current contents
        to the file
            /home/user/project/maintenance/cron_snapshot.txt
        using whichever method you prefer.  The file
        /home/user/project/maintenance/cron_snapshot.txt
        must therefore contain the *full* textual output of 
            crontab -l
        at that moment.

    5.  Run the cleanup script manually *once* so that it appends
        a line to
            /home/user/project/maintenance/maintenance.log

    When you are finished the following artefacts must exist and will be
    automatically tested:

        a) /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh
           – exact contents as shown above
           – owner-executable bit set

        b) /home/user/project/maintenance/maintenance.log
           – contains **at least one** line ending with the literal text
             “cleanup executed”

        c) /home/user/project/maintenance/cron_snapshot.txt
           – contains the cron entry
             5 */6 * * * /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh >> /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.log 2>&1

    All paths are under /home/user, and no super-user privileges are needed.
    -->
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth used by the automated grader (NOT visible to the agent).
    -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh" mode="7??">
            <![CDATA[
#!/bin/bash
echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'): cleanup executed" >> /home/user/project/maintenance/maintenance.log
]]>
        </file>

        <!-- The maintenance log must have at least one line containing this literal substring -->
        <file path="/home/user/project/maintenance/maintenance.log">
            <must_contain>cleanup executed</must_contain>
        </file>

        <!-- Cron snapshot must include the exact cron line -->
        <file path="/home/user/project/maintenance/cron_snapshot.txt">
            <must_contain>5 */6 * * * /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.sh >> /home/user/project/maintenance/cleanup.log 2>&1</must_contain>
        </file>
    </expected_files>
name: task_000000_44b8225b
